CP00-365 is a clean descriptor-only billing model-foundation-slice pack. I independently verified through read-only tools that:

- The 40-unit coverage and deliverable/micro-title distributions match across plan, baseline, manifest, contract, and code.
- The billing source is pure frozen-object descriptor generation with no IO, runtime, network, or real-data paths.
- The contract, validator, tests, and manifest are mutually consistent (deepEqual contract↔code enforcement).
- No-write/no-real-data boundaries, read-only Claude authority (not final approver), and Hermes-as-descriptor-only-not-runtime are all enforced.
- CP178+ review hardening holds: auth preflight passed (exit 0, non-empty valid JSON), no invalid attempts, only Read/Grep/Glob authorized.
- The pack does not over-claim — it is honestly labeled `production_ready: false` / `implemented_pending_review`, with enterprise trust correctly gated behind downstream human approval.

Independently verified via Read/Grep/Glob only: (1) Coverage is exactly 40 units with deliverable distribution {implementation:23, ui:5, fixture:2, test:6, hermes_evidence:2, claude_review:2} and micro-title distribution {Type And Shape Definition:12, Primary Implementation Slice:22, Secondary Workflow Slice:6}, matching plan, baseline, manifest, contract, and code requirements (confirmed 40 unit IDs and 40 per-unit implemented_pending_review statuses in the manifest). (2) The billing source (registry.js, service.js, validators.js, index.js) consists of pure frozen-object descriptor generators with no fs/net/http/child_process/process.env/fetch imports, no real data, and no writes. (3) The contract billing-core-contract.json is internally consistent (current_pack=CP00-365, model_foundation_slice_descriptor, cp365_requirements, cp365_no_write_attestation, no_write_attestation, historical_pack_bindings ending at CP00-365), and validate-rp12-billing-core-contract.mjs performs deepEqual checks between the stored contract and the code-generated descriptors/requirements/attestations. (4) No-write/no-real-data boundaries are comprehensively attested; Hermes patterns are used only as deterministic descriptor packets (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false / no_hermes_runtime_receipt:true), never product runtime. (5) Authority boundary is preserved: claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true; the manifest mirrors claude_not_final_approver:true and enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation:true. (6) CP178+ review hardening holds: the readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1432-byte non-empty stdout, well-formed JSON), invalid_attempts_preserved is empty, the forbidden_review_evidence list is present, and only Read/Grep/Glob are authorized. (7) The pack does not over-claim: it is honestly labeled production_ready:false, status implemented_pending_review, runtime_ready:false; production_ready_candidate:true denotes descriptor coverage/slice verification only, not an enterprise-trust or production claim. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present, which is expected because they are produced after this read-only review in the hardened sequence. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
